mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS remains below 0.02 over all 3-year rolling windows, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with rate declines.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 8.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and collapsing consumer spending power, which forces equity valuations lower over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent, or the signal fails to predict direction with p-value below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand recovery, which sustains corporate revenue growth and generates positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to yield a positive 60-day forward return in at least 60 percent of triggered cases, or interaction term IC is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during late-cycle expansions reflects capacity utilization nearing limits, which triggers inflation expectations and prompts risk-off positioning that depresses short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 5-day returns in over 50 percent of observations, or the regime-conditional signal loses predictive power in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month CPI inflation exceeding 0.5 percent forces central banks to consider tightening policy, which raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.5 percent, or the threshold signal shows no statistical edge.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates indicates robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields negative 60-day returns in over 55 percent of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate profit expectations, which translates into immediate positive market repricing and short-term S&P 500 gains over the next week.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no significant positive correlation with 5-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year industrial production contraction below zero percent signals severe supply-side disruption and falling economic output, which triggers risk aversion and negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of months where INDPRO contracts year-over-year, or the threshold condition fails to predict direction with p < 0.05.
